title: Assembly.GetExecutingAssembly should not be called
description: "Assembly.GetExecutingAssembly" should not be calledAnalyze your codeintentionality-efficientmaintainabilityCode SmellperformanceWhy is this an issue?UsingType.Assemblyto get the current assembly is nearly free in terms of performance; it’s a simple property access. On the other hand,Assembly.GetExecutingAssembly()can take up to 30 times as long because it walks up the call stack to find the assembly.Note thatAssembly.GetExecutingAssembly()is different thanType.Assemblybecause it dynamically returns the assembly that contains the startup object of the currently executed application. For example, if executed from an application it will return the application assembly, but if executed from a unit test project it could return the unit test assembly.Type.Assemblyalways returns the assembly tha
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

noncompliant: Assembly currentAssembly = Assembly.GetExecutingAssembly(); // Noncompliant - performance issue
compliant: Assembly currentAssembly = typeof(MyClass).Assembly; // Compliant - better performance

checks.message: Use typeof(YourClass).Assembly instead of Assembly.GetExecutingAssembly() for better performance.
